extraction.one_line_summary: SEPP KUSS attacked with 20km remaining on home roads in Andorra, holding off VALVERDE and the break remnants after INEOS pacing isolated POGACAR's rivals.
tactical_patterns: INEOS pulling DEKKER/CASTROVIEJO back from 32-rider break to pace GC group reveals team preservation over breakaway stage wins.
ROGLIC's team still winning mountain stages post-abandon shows JUMBO prioritized stage hunting over domestique conservation for GC riders.
CARAPAZ lost 3+ minutes on descent not climb, moving 2nd to 9th, exposing descending as critical selection point.
POGACAR never needed to respond to late WOUT attacks showing UAE isolation tactics working despite numerical disadvantage.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: attacked solo with 20km left on local Andorran roads, suggesting course knowledge provides measurable advantage
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: dropped on descent not climb, falling from 2nd to 9th, reveals technical weakness under pressure
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: outsprinted break remnants at age 41 for second, still dangerous in reduced bunch sprints
rider_signals.POELS Wout: positive: moved to KOM lead with 8-point gap via breakaway, showing UAE prioritizing secondary classifications
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: late attacks with VINGEGAARD failed to distress POGACAR, suggesting JUMBO lacked firepower for GC disruption
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: animated in break for Arkea's first stage but couldn't deliver, now hunting KOM jersey instead
